subject: LU-5242 tests: DEBUG ONLY Attempt to narrow failure window

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/29176/

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 
 
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/29176/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1:

Hopefully this patch allows isolating the root cause of the failure to a specific subtest.

However, if this patch with 10x sanity runs doesn't find the problem, we may have to approach it in s different way. It might be that unmounting after every test avoids whatever race condition is causing the problem. Also, this might cause the testing to be extremely slow. 

We know that the cause of the hang is somewhere before test_132() and the previous test that unmounts the OST (not sure which one that is right now), since this was the only test to report problems until it was disabled and we started seeing failures in test_133g().

It may be better to perturb the testing as little as possible and just insert a single unmount/remount of ost1 half way between test_132 and the previous unmount (or the start of sanity if that isn't clear). Then, we use the test results to bisect the remaining tests depending on whether the added unmount fails or not.

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

Patch is still in testing after 3 days, but I'm not sure how far along it is because all the tests are "sanity".  I guess the combination of unmount/remount for every subtest plus the number of iterations is causing this to run very slowly.  

If we need to run similar testing in the future it might be better to submit several separate patches so they can run in parallel.
Uploaded patch set 2.

commit.subject: LU-5242 tests: DEBUG ONLY Attempt to narrow failure window
commit.message:
LU-5242 tests: DEBUG ONLY Attempt to narrow failure window

Instead of trying to clean/setup between every test, try
only select places.

This Test: 80 81b 99
Prior pass: 50 60 70 80
Prior Fail: 100 132
